title: Calendar
subtitle: Component for selecting dates with multiple modes (day, week, range), sizes and width strategies

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: Simple calendar for single date selection. Displays the current month and allows navigating between months and years.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five size options (xs, sm, md, lg, xl) and a sixth auto option that adapts to the container. Sizes harmoniously scale all internal elements.
sizes.note: Sizes affect the width, cell height, padding and font size of the calendar. Use size with values 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg', 'xl' or 'auto'.

widths.title: Width strategies
widths.description: Two width strategies: compact (fixed width based on size) and full (occupies 100% of the container). Full mode uses Container Queries to adjust typography.
widths.note: Use width='compact' for a fixed-width calendar ideal for popovers. Use width='full' for a calendar that occupies all available space, ideal for forms or content areas.

week-selection.title: Week selection
week-selection.description: Special mode for selecting a full week (7 consecutive days). Useful for weekly planning.
week-selection.note: Set type='WEEK' to enable this mode. The calendar will highlight the complete week on hover and return an object with the start and end dates of the week.

range-selection.title: Range selection
range-selection.description: Allows selecting a date range with start and end. Visually displays the intermediate dates of the range.
range-selection.note: Set type='RANGE' to enable this mode. The calendar will wait for two clicks: one for the start and one for the end of the range. The output will return a { start: Date, end: Date } object.

presets.title: Quick range presets
presets.description: Add preset buttons for frequent ranges like 'Last week', 'Last month', 'Last year'. Supports Remix Icon icons.
presets.note: This feature is only available when type='RANGE'. Provide an array of DateRangePreset objects in customPresets. Each preset has a label, range (with start/end) and an optional icon.

time-picker.title: Time Picker integration
time-picker.description: Combines date selection with time selection. Supports start time, end time or both depending on the calendar type.
time-picker.note: Use showTimePicker='true' and configure timePickerMode with 'start', 'end' or 'both'. You can customize the time picker with timePickerConfig (steps, range, presets, etc.).

disabled-dates.title: Disabled dates
disabled-dates.description: Block specific dates preventing their selection. Useful for holidays, weekends or unavailable dates.
disabled-dates.note: Use disabledDates to specify an array of disabled dates. Disabled dates show a distinctive dimmed style and are not selectable.

min-max-dates.title: Minimum and maximum range
min-max-dates.description: Restricts the selectable date range using minimum and maximum values. Dates outside the range are disabled.
min-max-dates.note: Configure minDate and maxDate to set the limits. Users will not be able to navigate beyond these limits or select dates outside the range.

reactive-forms.title: Reactive forms
reactive-forms.description: Full integration with Angular reactive forms. Implements ControlValueAccessor for automatic synchronization.
reactive-forms.note: Use formControl or formControlName to integrate with Angular forms. The component handles synchronization, validation and state automatically. Supports all types (DAY, WEEK, RANGE) in forms.

dynamic-disabled-dates.title: Dynamically disabled dates
dynamic-disabled-dates.description: Disable dates according to complex business logic using a custom function. Overrides the static disabledDates array.
dynamic-disabled-dates.note: Use isDateEnabledFn to define dynamic enablement rules. The function receives a date and must return true if enabled or false if it should be disabled. Ideal for corporate calendars, booking systems or applications with complex availability logic.

date-status.title: Visual states (dateStatusFn)
date-status.description: Assigns visual states to dates to indicate business information: availability, priority, events, etc.
date-status.note: Use dateStatusFn to assign one of four states to each date: 'success' (green), 'info' (blue), 'warning' (amber) or 'danger' (red). States are displayed as a subtle color line at the top of the day. Return null for days with no special state.

selection-modes.title: Advanced selection modes
selection-modes.description: The calendar supports three specialized selection modes: normal day selection, month/year selection and year-only selection.
selection-modes.note: Each mode is optimized for a specific use case: type='day' for full date selection, type='month' for selecting month and year (ideal for monthly filters), and type='year' for selecting only the year (ideal for annual reports). month and year modes start directly in the corresponding view and close automatically upon selection if closeOnSelect is active.

multiple-selection.title: Multiple selection
multiple-selection.description: Allows selecting multiple dates, months or years with a simple click. Ideal for selecting working days, holidays or distributed events.
multiple-selection.note: Enable multiple selection mode with selection="multiple" combined with type='day', type='month' or type='year'. Users can click multiple elements to add/remove them from the selection (toggle). This mode never automatically closes the calendar, even if closeOnSelect is true, to allow continuous selection. The output returns an object with dates, months or years depending on the type.

week-numbers.title: ISO-8601 week numbers
week-numbers.description: Displays week numbers according to the ISO-8601 standard in a column to the left of the calendar. Useful for businesses, logistics and planning.
week-numbers.note: Enable week numbers with [showWeekNumbers]="true". Week numbers are clickable when type='week' or when type='day' and selection='multiple', allowing selecting the full week with a single click. The ISO-8601 standard defines that the week starts on Monday and that week 1 of the year is the first week containing at least 4 days of the new year.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Input properties to configure the behavior, appearance and restrictions of the calendar

selection.description: Selection mode: 'single' (one) or 'multiple' (several). Applies to DAY, MONTH and YEAR. Users can click multiple elements to add/remove them (toggle)

showPresets.description: Shows presets panel (only for RANGE type)

dateStatusFn.description: Function that assigns visual states to dates ('success', 'warning', 'danger', 'info')

isDateEnabledFn.description: Function that dynamically determines if a date is enabled (overrides disabledDates)

showWeekNumbers.description: Shows ISO-8601 week numbers in a column to the left. Clickable in WEEK mode or DAY multiple to select the full week

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the calendar when the user selects a date or range

valueChange.description: Emits when the selection changes. Includes date(s), range/week and time if enabled

calendar-value.title: CalendarValue
calendar-value.description: Union type that represents all possible calendar values depending on the selected type
calendar-value.note: The type of returned value depends on the type: DAY returns Date | null, WEEK returns { start: Date, end: Date } | null, and RANGE returns { start: Date, end: Date } | null.

calendar-type.title: CalendarType
calendar-type.description: Enum defining the calendar selection mode

DAY.description: Single date selection. With selection="multiple" allows selecting multiple dates

WEEK.description: Selection of a full week (7 consecutive days)

RANGE.description: Selection of a date range (start and end)

MONTH.description: Month and year selection. With selection="multiple" allows selecting multiple months

YEAR.description: Year selection. With selection="multiple" allows selecting multiple years

calendar-width.title: CalendarWidth
calendar-width.description: Enum defining the calendar width strategy

calendar-selection.title: CalendarSelection
calendar-selection.description: Enum defining the calendar selection mode: single or multiple

single.description: Allows selecting a single element (day, month or year depending on the type)

multiple.description: Allows selecting multiple elements with toggle on click. The calendar never closes automatically in this mode

view-mode.title: ViewMode
view-mode.description: Enum defining the current view mode (days, months or years). Primarily for internal component use.
view-mode.note: This enum is used internally by the component to manage navigation between views. It is not necessary to configure it directly in most cases.

DAY.description: Days view of the month (7x6 grid)

MONTH.description: Months view of the year (3x4 grid)

preset.title: DateRangePreset
preset.description: Interface for defining quick range presets with label, date range and optional icon

smart-types.title: Smart Types
smart-types.description: Types and functions for advanced date validation and status logic. Allow applying complex business rules and dynamic visualizations.
smart-types.note: These types are fundamental for implementing advanced features: DateStatusFn assigns visual states (success, warning, danger, info) to dates based on business logic, while IsDateEnabledFn dynamically controls which dates are enabled or disabled. Both functions receive a date and allow implementing complex validations such as corporate calendars, booking systems or custom availability rules.

DateStatusFn.description: (date: Date) => DateStatus | null — Function that dynamically assigns visual states

IsDateEnabledFn.description: (date: Date) => boolean — Function that validates if a date is enabled (overrides disabledDates)

colors.title: Colors
colors.description: The calendar primarily uses global theme variables. Selected days, hover and active states use the primary color of the active theme.
colors.note: The component fully integrates with the NUI theming system. Primary colors, background, text and borders are automatically inherited from the selected theme preset.

dayBg.description: Background color of the day cells

dayText.description: Text color of the day cells

dayHoverBg.description: Background color on day cell hover

dayHoverText.description: Text color on day cell hover

daySelectedBg.description: Background color of the selected day

daySelectedText.description: Text color of the selected day

daySelectedBorder.description: Border color of the selected day

daySelectedHoverBg.description: Background color of the selected day on hover

daySelectedHoverBorder.description: Border color of the selected day on hover

dayRangeBg.description: Background color of days within a range

dayRangeText.description: Text color of days within a range

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Size-specific CSS variables that control width, padding, spacing, cell size and typography. Five size variants available: xs, sm, md (default), lg and xl.

titleFontSize.description: Font size of the month/year title

weekdayFontSize.description: Font size of weekday name headers

weekdayHeight.description: Height of the weekday name row

structure.title: Structure
structure.description: Structural CSS variables to control borders, opacities, animations and dimensions. Includes customization of tabs, days from other months, presets and focus states.

dayBorderWidth.description: Border thickness of the day cells

dayOtherMonthOpacity.description: Opacity of days from other months

presetBorderWidth.description: Border thickness of the preset buttons

customization.title: Customization
customization.description: Examples of how to customize calendar styles in your application. You can override CSS variables globally or specific to instances.

navigation.title: Navigation and label tokens
navigation.description: Tokens for day-of-week names, month names, navigation button labels and day state labels.
navigation.note: All array tokens (weekDaysShort, weekDays, months, shortMonths) must provide all values. Override globally with provideNuiI18n({ calendar: {...} }) or per instance with the [i18n] input.

weekDaysShort.description: Abbreviated day names displayed in the calendar header (Monday–Sunday).

weekDays.description: Full day names used in accessible labels and tooltips.

months.description: Full month names used in the month selector and accessible labels.

shortMonths.description: Abbreviated month names used in compact contexts.

prevMonth.description: Label for the button that navigates to the previous month.

nextMonth.description: Label for the button that navigates to the next month.

prevYears.description: Label for the button that navigates to previous years in the year selector.

nextYears.description: Label for the button that navigates to next years in the year selector.

changeMonth.description: Label for the button that opens the month selector.

changeYear.description: Label for the button that opens the year selector.

year.description: Label for the year selector header.

week.description: Accessible label for the week number header column.

weekNumbers.description: Tooltip for the week number column toggle button.

selectDay.description: Accessible description for selecting a day.

dayDisabled.description: Screen reader announcement for disabled day cells.

openCalendar.description: Label for the button that opens the calendar popover.

showTime.description: Label for the button that shows the time picker.

hideTime.description: Label for the button that hides the time picker.

monthAria.description: Accessible label for the month selection grid.

yearsAria.description: Accessible label for the year selection grid.

presetsAndTabs.title: Preset, tab and time picker tokens
presetsAndTabs.description: Tokens for quick access to predefined date ranges, view tabs and integrated time picker labels.

shortcuts.description: Header label for the presets panel that lists quick date range shortcuts.

last7Days.description: Label for the 'Last 7 days' quick preset.

last30Days.description: Label for the 'Last 30 days' quick preset.

thisMonth.description: Label for the 'This month' quick preset.

lastMonth.description: Label for the 'Last month' quick preset.

thisYear.description: Label for the 'This year' quick preset.

tabCalendar.description: Label for the calendar view tab.

tabPresets.description: Label for the presets view tab.

tabTime.description: Label for the time view tab.

timeStart.description: Label for the start time input in range mode with time.

timeEnd.description: Label for the end time input in range mode with time.

ariaTokens.title: State and accessibility ARIA tokens
ariaTokens.description: Tokens exposed only to assistive technologies to announce day states, navigation changes and range selection.

ariaToday.description: Screen reader annotation added to today's day cell.

selected.description: Screen reader annotation added to selected day cells.

inRange.description: Screen reader annotation added to day cells within a selected range.

ariaDisabled.description: Screen reader annotation added to disabled day cells.

statusSuccess.description: Screen reader annotation for dates with success status.

statusWarning.description: Screen reader annotation for dates with warning status.

statusDanger.description: Screen reader annotation for dates with danger status.

statusInfo.description: Screen reader annotation for dates with informational status.

calendarLabel.description: Accessible label for the calendar grid (role="grid").

keyboardInstructions.description: Instructions announced to screen reader users on how to navigate the calendar with the keyboard.

monthNavigated.description: Live announcement when the visible month changes. Supports {month} and {year} placeholders.

rangeStart.description: Live announcement when the start of a date range is selected.

rangeComplete.description: Live announcement when a complete date range has been selected.

weekNumberLabel.description: Accessible label for each week number cell. Supports the {week} placeholder.

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: The Calendar implements the ARIA Grid pattern for day cells and a live region for navigation announcements.

grid.description: The day grid uses role="grid" to enable arrow key navigation between cells.

gridLabel.description: The grid receives its label from the i18n token calendar.a11y.calendarLabel.

columnHeader.description: Weekday headers use role="columnheader" with the full day name as the accessible label.

liveRegion.description: A hidden aria-live="polite" region announces month navigation to screen readers.

naming.title: Accessible name
naming.description: The calendar grid and each day cell have descriptive accessible names derived from i18n tokens and the formatted date.
naming.note: Day cells include state annotations (today, selected, in range, disabled) added to their label via calendar.aria.* tokens.

grid.description: The calendar grid receives its accessible name from the calendar.a11y.calendarLabel token.

dayButton.description: The accessible name of each day button is the formatted date (e.g.: 'Tuesday, December 10, 2024').

stateAnnotation.description: State tokens (calendar.aria.today, selected, etc.) are added to the date label to communicate state to screen readers.

keyboard.title: Keyboard interaction
keyboard.description: The calendar day grid implements full keyboard navigation according to the ARIA Grid pattern.

home.description: Moves focus to the first day of the current week.

end.description: Moves focus to the last day of the current week.

escape.description: Closes the calendar popover and returns focus to the activator.

setup.title: Global Configuration
setup.description: The Calendar component supports global configuration via provideNUI(). This allows you to define default values once at the application level, avoiding repetition.
setup.note: Global configuration acts as default values and can always be overridden per instance using component inputs.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: The following properties can be configured globally via CalendarGlobalConfig:

rows.firstDayOfWeek: First day of the week. 0 = Sunday, 1 = Monday, etc.
rows.closeOnSelect: Whether the popover closes automatically when a date is selected.
rows.showTodayButton: Whether to display the 'Today' shortcut button.
rows.timePickerMode: Time picker precision mode (12/24 hours, with or without seconds).
rows.timePickerConfig: Advanced configuration object for the time picker.
rows.customPresets: List of custom date presets shown as shortcuts.
rows.defaultDate: Default date displayed when no value is selected.
rows.startTime: Default start time for range selections with time.
rows.endTime: Default end time for range selections with time.

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: NUI resolves configuration values using the following priority chain (highest wins):
